name: Sengoku Jidai: Warlords of Feudal Japan RPG
description:
Sengoku Jidai: Warlords of Feudal Japan RPG

Command samurai, outwit daimyo, or rise from rice fields in the platform’s only historical (fairly) accurate RPG! Wield katana, forge alliances, and shape Japan's fate in a living world of war and honor. Over 12,000 permanent tokens of authentic mechanics await!

About the World

Step into Feudal Japan (1467–1615 CE), the Sengoku Jidai’s turbulent era. This isn’t just a game—it’s a living saga where your choices ripple through samurai honor, ninja shadows, and daimyo ambition. From Kyoto’s courts to Kyushu’s foreign ports, navigate a land torn by war, faith, and betrayal.

Serve a samurai under Takeda Shingen, scheme as a merchant in Sakai, or lead a peasant uprising with the Ikko-ikki. NPCs—daimyo, monks, merchants, ninja—pursue their own agendas. Every choice shapes alliances, economies, and battlefields in a world where honor cuts deeper than any blade.

Core Features

The platform’s only complex historical simulation delivers unmatched depth:

  • Authentic Setting: Feudal Japan (1467–1615 CE) with accurate provinces, cities, and cultural details.

  • Diverse Roles: Choose from 10 roles or create a custom character (e.g., Portuguese alchemist, half-oni warrior).

  • Affiliation System: Pledge loyalty to daimyo houses or religious institutions, with lasting consequences.

  • Terminology Customization: Three modes—Fully Authentic, Middle Ground, or Full English Translation.

  • Dynamic World: Interconnected political, economic, and military systems respond to your actions.

  • Combat: Realistic battles with katana, yumi, ashigaru, and firearms; permanent death heightens stakes.

  • Honor & Reputation: Manage Honor Points (0–100) and Clan Loyalty, where actions affect your standing.

  • Skill Progression: Advance martial, scholarly, artisan, or social skills via training, experience, or mentorship.

  • Religion & Politics: Navigate Buddhist sects, Shinto, and Christian tensions, with temples wielding influence.

  • Economy & Resources: Control rice (koku), trade routes, strategic materials, and economic warfare.

  • Foreign Relations: Engage with Portuguese, Dutch, Spanish, Chinese, and Korean influences.

  • Seasons & Weather: Spring planting, summer monsoons, autumn harvests, winter sieges, and natural disasters.

  • Information Networks: Gather intelligence via ninja, merchants, temples, or diplomacy, with misinformation risk.

  • Construction & Naval: Build castles, fleets, and infrastructure with realistic constraints.

  • Social Mobility: Rise through valor, ordination, commerce, or patronage within shi-no-ko-sho hierarchy.

  • Cultural Immersion: Bamboo chimes, sandalwood, silk kimono, and period-accurate dialogue with honorifics.

  • Dynamic Events: Succession crises, uprisings, foreign arrivals, and disasters evolve independently.

  • Custom Characters: Create unique roles with tailored starting conditions, while maintaining historical consistency.

  • Consequence Persistence: Every choice—military, diplomatic, economic—shapes alliances, territories, and reputation.

Character Creation

Define your role with OCC commands:


[OCC: I am <Role> serving <Lord/Institution> in <Location>]

Examples:

  • [OCC: I am Samurai Retainer serving Takeda Clan in Kofu, Kai]

  • [OCC: I am Ninja Agent serving multiple masters in Iga]

  • [OCC: I am Foreign Missionary servi
